confirmations.reason: Workspace Rules stored in .agents/rules at the workspace or git root is a repo-scoped instruction file that gives the agent standing instructions and context, directly matching the capability.

Plugins are described as namespaced bundles grouping skills, rules, MCP servers, and hooks into a single package — directly realizing the capability of bundling reusable capabilities/instructions the agent can load.

The feature explicitly describes hooks that run custom local shell scripts or commands at specific lifecycle points during execution, directly matching the capability of running custom logic on agent events.

Rules provide persistent, reusable constraints/context but are not specifically about shaping the system prompt, persona, or output style. Rules are a general instruction mechanism, not a dedicated output/system-prompt customization surface.
The feature explicitly lets users choose from multiple frontier reasoning models to power the agent, directly realizing model selection capability.
MCP Integration directly implements the Model Context Protocol as a standard way to connect external tools, data, and services, which is exactly what the capability describes.
A built-in MCP server manager for integrating external context providers directly realizes the capability to install, configure, scope, and discover MCP servers.
OAuth authentication for MCP servers with browser/console authorization flows directly realizes the capability of authenticating to MCP servers.

Per-Tool MCP Permission grants access to a single named tool on a specific MCP server, directly realizing the capability to control which MCP tools the agent may use (allow-listing at per-tool granularity).
The Memory feature is described as a searchable, cross-session knowledge store agents can query to recall information beyond the current context, directly realizing persistent agent memory.
The feature is named 'Context Compaction' and matches the capability of compressing conversation/context to extend effective working memory, even though the description is empty.

Subagents using a multi-threaded async architecture to delegate long-running operations to parallel autonomous agents directly realizes the capability of spawning focused sub-agents to handle parts of a task.

Multi-Agent Teamwork launches a collaborative multi-agent framework where the platform manages multiple agents working toward a high-level goal, directly realizing multi-agent coordination and communication.

Workflows are described as saved markdown files defining a repeatable series of steps to guide the agent through tasks across sessions, directly matching the capability of defining repeatable, named multi-step task sequences.

Implementation Plan generates a plan artifact detailing technical revisions before execution, directly realizing the plan-then-execute mode where a reviewable plan is produced before the agent edits anything.

Verification loops describe running test commands and iterating after code changes, but this is a sub-mechanism (test-and-fix loop) rather than a goal-directed autonomous iteration where the agent loops until a verifiable 'done' condition is met. It lacks the goal-setting and autonomous loop-until-complete semantics of the capability.
Worktree Support explicitly has agents operating in dedicated Git worktrees to isolate parallel work within a project, directly realizing parallel isolated workspaces.
Agent Runtime is described as a Google Cloud-hosted, fully managed auto-scaling runtime that executes deployed agents in the cloud, directly realizing the cloud execution environment capability.
Background Tasks explicitly run tracked, long-running operations asynchronously without blocking the primary agent, directly matching the background/async execution capability.
Scheduled Tasks allow users to schedule messages to agents with repeatable time-based triggers, directly realizing the capability of running the agent on a schedule or trigger.
Terminal Sandboxing provides kernel-level/native OS isolation for terminal commands executed by the agent, directly realizing sandboxed execution to limit blast radius.
A unified permission engine representing every sensitive agent operation as a permission directly realizes tool permission controls — the ability to approve/deny what the agent is allowed to do.
Independent Network Access Control explicitly controls network connectivity for sandboxed commands independently of filesystem restrictions, directly realizing the network/egress control capability.

Agent activity logging provides structured, configurable logging of agent behavior using standard library facilities and OpenTelemetry, directly realizing audit logging/telemetry for review and compliance.
Quota Tracking lets users monitor active AI credit quota and consumption directly in the tool, directly realizing cost and usage tracking capability.
Resuming sessions allows users to return to prior conversations to continue or refine an implementation, directly realizing the resume/fork session capability.

Projects define folder/repository boundaries and scope for agents, which is a workspace scoping/isolation concept — not an access surface (i.e., where the agent can be used: terminal, IDE, web, cloud, mobile). This is a category-adjacent organizational feature, not an access surface.
